data.title: Ming Fai Announces Final HKD 0.04 Dividend for 2025
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ming Fai International Holdings ( (HK:3828) ) has provided an update.

Ming Fai International Holdings Limited has declared a final ordinary dividend of HKD 0.04 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, subject to shareholder approval at the meeting scheduled for 20 May 2026. The shares will trade ex-dividend on 22 May 2026, with the record date set for 29 May 2026 and payment due on 5 June 2026, signalling the company’s continued commitment to shareholder returns and offering income clarity to investors.

The dividend will be paid in Hong Kong dollars with no withholding tax applicable, and Computershare Hong Kong Investor Services Limited will act as the share registrar overseeing entitlement and registration logistics. The announcement, which also confirms the current composition of the board including executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reinforces transparency for stakeholders tracking governance and payout policies.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3828) stock is a Buy with a HK$1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ming Fai International Holdings stock, see the HK:3828 Stock Forecast page.

More about Ming Fai International Holdings

Ming Fai International Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company engaged in the manufacture and supply of consumer and hospitality products, serving customers in both local and international markets. The group focuses on delivering value to shareholders while maintaining compliance with Hong Kong listing regulations and corporate governance standards.

Average Trading Volume: 495,758

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$612.2M

data.title: Da Ming International Formalizes Nomination Committee Governance and Diversity Mandate
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from Da Ming International Holdings Limited ( (HK:1090) ).

Da Ming International Holdings Limited has formalized the terms of reference for its Nomination Committee, detailing its composition, meeting procedures, and governance framework to strengthen board oversight. The committee, made up predominantly of independent non-executive directors and including at least one member of a different gender, is tasked with reviewing the board’s structure, size, and diversity, maintaining a skills matrix, and identifying suitably qualified candidates for directorships.

The Nomination Committee will meet at least annually, with the company secretary acting as secretary and maintaining minutes that are circulated to both committee and board members. By embedding clear processes for board renewal and diversity in areas such as gender, age, background, and professional experience, the company aims to align its governance structure more closely with its corporate strategy and stakeholder expectations.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1090) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.82 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Da Ming International Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:1090 Stock Forecast page.

More about Da Ming International Holdings Limited

Da Ming International Holdings Limited operates in the industrial and manufacturing sector, overseen by a board of directors that emphasizes structured governance. The company utilizes specialized committees, including a Nomination Committee composed mainly of independent non-executive directors, to manage board composition and support its corporate strategy and diversity objectives.

data.title: Xiabuxiabu Cuts Loss-Making Stores as Revenue Falls Amid Fierce Catering Competition
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Xiabuxiabu Catering Management (China) Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:0520) ) has shared an announcement.

Xiabuxiabu Catering Management reported a sharp revenue decline of 20.3% in 2025 to RMB3.79 billion, alongside a narrowed but still substantial annual loss attributable to shareholders of RMB300.5 million. The group opened 57 new restaurants but closed 109 underperforming outlets, reflecting a strategic shift toward pruning loss-making sites and realigning locations with its value-for-money positioning amid intense industry competition and more cautious consumer spending.

As of year-end, the company operated 755 Xiabuxiabu and 131 Coucou restaurants in Mainland China, plus a small number of overseas stores, giving it significant scale in a catering market that is recovering only moderately. Management expects that ongoing portfolio adjustments and efficiency measures will help improve restaurant profitability and bolster its position as industry consolidation accelerates, as weaker operators exit and leading brands explore leaner formats and selective overseas expansion.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0520) stock is a Sell with a HK$0.71 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Xiabuxiabu Catering Management (China) Holdings Co., Ltd. stock, see the HK:0520 Stock Forecast page.

More about Xiabuxiabu Catering Management (China) Holdings Co., Ltd.

Xiabuxiabu Catering Management (China) Holdings Co., Ltd. is a China-based casual dining group operating the Xiabuxiabu and Coucou restaurant brands, primarily focused on hotpot and related catering services. The company runs hundreds of outlets across multiple provinces and major municipalities in Mainland China, as well as a growing but still limited presence in overseas markets.

The latest announcement is out from Wasion Holdings Limited ( (HK:3393) ).

Wasion Holdings Limited reported solid growth for the year ended 31 December 2025, with turnover rising 16% year-on-year to RMB10.07 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders surging 50% to RMB1.06 billion. data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Zhongsheng Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:0881) ) has provided an announcement.

Zhongsheng Group reported a 2.2% decline in total revenue to RMB164.4 billion for 2025, as lower sales of motor vehicles and accessories offset growth in after-sales services. New car sales volumes rose 2.5%, driven by a 6.2% increase in luxury brand units, while pre-owned trade volumes slipped slightly.

Profitability deteriorated sharply, with total gross profit down 17.2%, commission income falling 38.7% and the group swinging to a net loss of RMB1.9 billion versus a prior-year profit. The results highlight margin pressure in vehicle sales and financial services, even as after-sales operations continue to expand, underscoring a shifting revenue mix and potential challenges for returns to shareholders.

The latest announcement is out from Shenzhen International Holdings ( (HK:0152) ).

Shenzhen International Holdings reported 2025 revenue of HK$16.35 billion, up from HK$15.57 billion a year earlier, but saw gross profit edge down to HK$3.44 billion as costs rose. Operating profit slipped slightly to HK$5.10 billion, and earnings attributable to ordinary shareholders fell to HK$2.25 billion, with basic earnings per share declining to HK$0.93.

Profit for the year dropped to HK$2.94 billion from HK$3.69 billion, reflecting lower contributions from associates and higher tax charges, although net finance costs improved. Despite the earnings decline, total comprehensive income surged to HK$5.79 billion, driven largely by favourable foreign exchange translation effects, which may soften the perceived impact of weaker core profitability for investors and other stakeholders.

Flowing Cloud Technology Ltd. ( (HK:6610) ) has issued an update.

Flowing Cloud Technology Ltd. reported a sharp deterioration in its 2025 results, as revenue fell 21.2% to RMB783.9 million and gross profit dropped 23.9%, while its loss before tax ballooned to RMB365.6 million from RMB44.8 million a year earlier. The company attributed its performance to the challenging dynamics of China’s rapidly growing but highly competitive AR&VR market, where high production costs, limited consumer adoption and global technology constraints are weighing on profitability.

Despite the tougher environment, the group’s main revenue driver remained AR&VR marketing services delivered through partnerships with media platforms, complemented by bespoke AR, VR and AI content solutions for clients in multiple sectors. In 2025 it also launched a custom AI software platform offering text, image, visual and video generation, a move that could enhance its service mix and operational efficiency as it seeks to strengthen its position in immersive digital marketing and content creation.

Peijia Medical Ltd. ( (HK:9996) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Peijia Medical Limited reported that its chairman, chief executive officer and executive director, Dr. Yi Zhang, plans to increase his stake in the company by purchasing shares on the open market through a controlled corporation starting March 26, 2026. The planned share purchases will have a total consideration not exceeding HK$15 million and will be executed in compliance with applicable laws and regulations.

According to the company, Dr. Zhang views the additional investment as a demonstration of his confidence in Peijia Medical’s intrinsic value, development prospects and growth potential, and he may further increase his holdings in future. The company also stressed that, even after the shareholding increase, it continues to maintain sufficient public float under Hong Kong listing rules and advised shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading its shares.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Meitu ( (HK:1357) ) has shared an announcement.

Meitu, Inc. has moved to address recent online forum posts that referenced unpublished figures for its annual results for the year ended December 31, 2025, information the company classifies as inside information. The company emphasized that its annual results remain subject to approval by its board of directors at a meeting scheduled for March 27, 2026.

At Meitu’s request, trading in its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange was halted from 9 a.m. on March 26, 2026, ahead of the planned publication of its 2025 annual results on March 27. The move underscores the company’s efforts to manage disclosure of price-sensitive information and maintain orderly trading amid unverified market speculation online.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1357) stock is a Buy with a HK$16.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Meitu stock, see the HK:1357 Stock Forecast page.

More about Meitu

Meitu, Inc. is a Hong Kong–based technology company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong that develops and operates image processing and related digital services. The company focuses on consumer-facing photo and video editing applications and associated platforms, targeting users in China and international markets through mobile and online channels.
